---
title: "Virginia Marsh St. John's-Wort (Hypericum virginicum)"
slug: "hypericum-virginicum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622246780/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Virginia Marsh St. John's-Wort"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malpighiales"
tax_family: "Hypericaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malpighiales", "Hypericaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622246780/medium.jpg" alt="Virginia Marsh St. John's-Wort" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/ism14">
ism14</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341771526">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malpighiales
-   **Family:** Hypericaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=859236)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and seepage areas.

## Natural history notes

A small wetland wildflower characterized by its narrow leaves and pale pink to whitish flowers with numerous fine stamens. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as bogs, wet flatwoods, and seepage areas, often in sandy, acidic soils. It typically blooms during the warmer months and is a subtle component of wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/859236-Hypericum-virginicum)
-   [Local profile](https://www.fnps.org/plant/triadenum-virginicum#gsc.tab=0)
